title: WIP - Update rpm spec file in contrib to pass rpmlint
body:
Recent changes to the RPM spec file resulted in failure to pass rpmlint validation which was breaking `rpmbuild`. This update fixes minor issues so the spec file once again passes `rpmlint`.

Signed-off-by: pixdrift <support@pixeldrift.net>

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

I am concerned this is going to impact the release script which does string replacement in the spec file off buildah version. As I started digging, it looked like it went deeper than just the spec.

The primary problem is that having the hyphen in the version isn't valid, everything else is cosmetic (changelog dates need to be valid to pass lint).

@TomSweeneyRedHat thoughts on this?

Happy to move it back to WIP until we figure out approach.
@pixdrift has some spec changed to disallow a hyphen/dash (-) in a version number somewhere?  I'd like to keep *-dev if possible.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably d8fe400) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@pixdrift @TomSweeneyRedHat What is up with this PR?  Is it still needed?
I'm going to close this for now.  @pixdrift feel free to reopen or spin up a new PR if you think we need changes still.
